title: Profit for the biggest U.S. oil companies declined in 1st quarter – but only on paper
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The largest U.S. oil companies, Exxon and Chevron, saw a decline in reported profits for the first quarter, primarily due to financial hedges that backfired ami
excerpt: The largest U.S. oil companies, Exxon and Chevron, saw a decline in reported profits for the first quarter, primarily due to financial hedges that backfired amid geopolitical tensions. Despite this, their actual earnings remained strong as crude and gasoline prices surged. The reported drop was larg

content.introduction: The largest U.S. oil companies, Exxon and Chevron, saw a decline in reported profits for the first quarter, primarily due to financial hedges that backfired amid geopolitical tensions. Despite this, their actual earnings remained strong as crude and gasoline prices surged. The reported drop was largely a paper loss.
body: Exxon earned $4.18 billion, or $1 per share, for the period ended March 31. A year earlier it earned $7.7 billion, or $1.76 per share. The
Exxon Mobil's profit declined in the first quarter, stung partly by weaker crude prices and higher costs. By Associated Press.
Overall, net income dropped to $2.35 billion, the lowest in three years. “It was a tough market environment for us this quarter,” said Exxon
